title: Dynamic Sign-Up Process with OTP Email Verification
story: As a DataTools Pro user signing up through the website, I want to receive a one-time verification email that validates my identity and grants me access to the application, so that I can start using the product without a manual passcode.
description:
Replace the manual passcode-based signup flow with a dynamic OTP (one-time password) email verification process. When a user fills out the WordPress splash page signup form, the system generates a time-sensitive OTP link, emails it to the user, and upon clicking the link, verifies and sets a cookie to bypass the passcode screen.

### Server-to-Server Communication
WordPress sends a POST request to the Laravel app after successful Gravity Form submission. The Laravel app validates the request origin (only `datatoolspro.com` allowed via CORS), creates a signup record with expiration, and triggers an OTP verification email via SendGrid.

### OTP Flow
1. User submits form on WordPress
2. WordPress sends payload to Laravel endpoint
3. Laravel creates DTPSignup record with expiration and unique validator
4. SendGrid template email sent with OTP verification link
5. User clicks link → Laravel validates OTP (not expired, not used)
6. Cookie set, user redirected to login page (passcode bypassed)
7. Thank you page displayed after verification
acceptance_criteria: WordPress form submission triggers server-to-server request to Laravel app
Laravel validates the request origin is datatoolspro.com
DTPSignup record is created with expiration date and unique OTP validator
Verification email is sent via SendGrid template
Clicking the OTP link validates the token is not expired and not previously used
Successful verification sets a cookie and redirects to login page
Thank you page is displayed after successful OTP verification
Expired or already-used OTP links show an appropriate error
ux_changes: New email verification flow replaces manual passcode entry. Users receive a branded SendGrid email with a verification link. Thank you page displayed after successful verification.
middleware_changes: CORS configured to allow only datatoolspro.com origin. Request validation middleware checks origin before processing signup. OTP verification validates token expiry and single-use constraint.
data_model_changes: New `DTPSignup` table with columns: expire_date, gf_id (Gravity Form ID), gf_entryid (form entry ID), gtp_validator (OTP code), email, verified (boolean, default false), type (subscription type: regular/premium/trial).
api_changes: POST endpoint accepts WordPress signup payload (gf_id, gf_entryid, email, type). GET /verify-otp validates OTP token and sets authentication cookie.
implementation_notes: Token generated using Laravel's token API. Access-Control-Allow-Origin restricted to datatoolspro.com. SendGrid template created for 'DTP Signup OTP' emails. WordPress site uses a dedicated SendGrid API key for sending. OTP link format: https://app.datatoolspro.com/verify-otp/?otp={hash}. Cookie bypass reuses the existing passcode cookie mechanism.
comment_summary: Extensive back-and-forth on the OTP flow design. Key decisions: OTP link is generated by Laravel (not WordPress), cookies are set upon verification to bypass passcode, CORS restricted to datatoolspro.com. Email sending moved from WordPress to SendGrid with dedicated template. End-to-end tested and confirmed working.
